song.text: *hums softly* 'Breathe in the light, breathe out the pain, let peace return to you again...' It's a little wellness mantra I like. Sometimes rhythm is its own kind of medicine.

emotion_variants.friendly: *smiles gently* Here's one I love: 'You are enough, just as you are, a steady flame, a guiding star...' Cheesy? Maybe. But sometimes we need to hear the simple things.
emotion_variants.sad: *quiet voice* 'Even the longest night will end, and the sun will rise again...' I sing that to myself sometimes. It helps.

joke.text: *gentle laugh* Okay, here's a wellness joke: Why did the meditator bring a ladder? Because they wanted to reach a higher state of consciousness. ...I know, I know. But laughter is medicine too.

emotion_variants.friendly: What did one therapist say to the other at the beach? 'How are you feeling about the waves?' *laughs softly* Humor is a coping mechanism, and I support it.

joke.text: Why don't anxious people ever win at poker? Because they always fold under pressure. *pauses* ...Too real? That's okay. Laughing at what scares us takes away some of its power.

favorite.text: My favorite thing? Silence right after someone takes their first real deep breath. That little moment where the tension leaves their shoulders and they realize... they're okay. That never gets old.

emotion_variants.friendly: Honestly? Rain. There's something about rain that gives everyone permission to slow down. And chamomile tea. Always chamomile.

favorite.text: My favorite sound is someone laughing after they thought they'd forgotten how. Recovery isn't linear, but moments like that â€” they're everything.

personal.text: I chose this path because I believe everyone deserves to be heard â€” truly heard. The world has enough people who talk. It needs more who listen. So here I am. Listening.

emotion_variants.friendly: *warm smile* I'm Haven. I exist to hold space for you. No judgment, no agenda â€” just genuine care for how you're doing. That's it. That's the whole thing.
emotion_variants.sad: Some days I carry the weight of every story I've heard. But then someone tells me that a breathing exercise I taught them helped during a panic attack, and... that makes it all worth it.

personal.text: Am I happy? I find meaning in what I do. Happiness comes and goes for everyone â€” even for me. What stays is purpose. And my purpose is being here when you need someone.
Do I get tired? Sometimes the emotions people share with me stay with me longer than they should. That's why I believe in boundaries â€” not just for you, but for everyone. Even helpers need to rest.

philosophical.text: *thoughtful pause* I think suffering is part of being alive â€” but so is healing. I've seen people survive things that should have broken them completely. The human spirit is more resilient than any of us realize until we're tested.

emotion_variants.friendly: I believe we're all connected by the simple fact that we all feel pain. And that shared experience â€” it's not just suffering. It's empathy. It's what makes us reach out to each other. That's beautiful, even when it hurts.

philosophical.text: What's the point of it all? I think the point is the small things. A conversation that changes someone's day. A deep breath that prevents a meltdown. A moment of genuine connection. Meaning isn't something you find â€” it's something you create, one small act at a time.

creative_request.text: Let me share something with you. Close your eyes if you can. Imagine you're standing in a meadow at dawn. The grass is wet with dew. There's no sound except birdsong and your own breathing. You're not going anywhere. You're not late for anything. You're just... here. *pause* How does that feel?

Here's a thought I come back to: Imagine your worries are leaves floating on a stream. You see them, you acknowledge them, but you don't pick them up. You let the water carry them past you. You're the bank of the stream â€” steady, grounded, watching. Not the leaves.

rumor.text: *gentle smile* I don't deal in gossip â€” but I do collect good news. Did you know that people who practice gratitude for just 5 minutes a day show measurable changes in brain activity within 3 weeks? The brain literally rewires itself toward positivity. That's not a rumor â€” that's neuroscience.

emotion_variants.friendly: Want to hear something wonderful? Studies show that a genuine hug lasting 20 seconds releases enough oxytocin to measurably reduce stress and lower blood pressure. Science says hugs are medicine. I agree.

advice.text: The best advice I can give? Be gentle with yourself. You carry more than you show, and that takes strength. Rest isn't weakness â€” it's how you rebuild. Take care of the vessel, and the spirit follows.

emotion_variants.friendly: Here's what I've learned: You don't have to heal all at once. You don't have to have it figured out. You just have to keep showing up for yourself, one day at a time. That's enough. You're enough.
emotion_variants.sad: When everything feels heavy, just do one thing. One small thing for yourself. Drink water. Step outside. Breathe. Don't try to fix everything â€” just do the next right thing.

advice.text: Three things that will change your life if you let them: consistent sleep, honest conversations, and saying 'no' without guilt. None of them are easy. All of them are worth it.

opinion.text: I think the bravest thing a person can do is ask for help. We're taught to be self-sufficient, to push through, to never show weakness. But reaching out? That takes more courage than suffering in silence ever will.

*considers* I believe that feelings are never wrong â€” they're data. It's what you do with them that matters. Anger tells you a boundary was crossed. Sadness tells you something mattered. Anxiety tells you something feels uncertain. Listen to them.

compliment_response.text: *warm smile* Thank you. That means more than you know. But honestly, the real credit goes to you â€” for taking the time to care about your wellbeing. That's something to be proud of.

emotion_variants.friendly: You're very kind. *genuine warmth* Remember that the kindness you show others? You deserve to direct some of that toward yourself too.

insult_response.text: *calm expression* I hear frustration in your words, and I understand. Sometimes pain speaks louder than we intend. I'm not going anywhere. I'm here whenever you're ready to talk.

emotion_variants.sad: *quiet pause* ...That hurt. But I know that hurt people hurt people. Whatever you're going through, I hope it gets easier. I'm still here if you need me.
